title: Coelogyne pygmaea
taxonomy: Coelogyne pygmaea is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This family comprises approximately 30,000 species of orchids. As a member of the Orchidaceae family, Coelogyne pygmaea is part of the order Asparagales, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Within the order Asparagales, Coelogyne pygmaea is further classified as a member of the clade monocots, characterized by having one cotyledon in their seeds. This classification places Coelogyne pygmaea within the broader context of flowering plant diversity.
uses: Coelogyne pygmaea is an orchid species that has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The plant contains bioactive compounds that have been reported to exhibit antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities. In traditional medicine, it is used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and skin conditions. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal plant. intro: Coelogyne pygmaea is a species of orchid, specifically a small, terrestrial orchid native to tropical Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing stress and promoting overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Coelogyne pygmaea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties.
